frontmatter.title: Non-negatively generated basis yields positive generation

mainmatter.p: Let A be a pointed continuous dcpo presented by a basis B \subseteq A such that each element of B is non-negatively generated in A. Then A is positively generated.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: We must show that for any a \in A, we have a \sqsubseteq \bigsqcup _{p: \mathsf { pos } \, a}a. Let B_a be the subset { \mathopen {} \left \{ b \in B \mid b \ll a \right \} \mathclose {}}. By assumption, it suffices to check that \bigsqcup B_a \sqsubseteq \bigsqcup _{p: \mathsf { pos } \, a}a. Fixing b \in B_a, we must check that b \sqsubseteq \bigsqcup _{p: \mathsf { pos } \, a}a.

As b is assumed to be non-negatively generated, we have b= \bigsqcup _{p: \lnot { \mathopen {} \left ( b= \bot \right ) \mathclose {}} }b so to check b \sqsubseteq \bigsqcup _{p: \mathsf { pos } \, a}a it suffices to check that \lnot { \mathopen {} \left ( b= \bot \right ) \mathclose {}} implies that a is positive. We fix semidirected J \subseteq A such that a \sqsubseteq \bigsqcup {J} = \bigsqcup { \mathopen {} \left ( { \mathopen {} \left \{ \bot \right \} \mathclose {}} \cup J \right ) \mathclose {}} to check that J is inhabited. We recall that b \ll a and a \sqsubseteq \bigsqcup { \mathopen {} \left ( { \mathopen {} \left \{ \bot \right \} \mathclose {}} \cup J \right ) \mathclose {}}, so it follows by the definition of the way-below relation that either b \sqsubseteq \bot or there exists j \in J such that b \sqsubseteq j. The first case is contradictory by our assumption that \lnot { \mathopen {} \left ( b= \bot \right ) \mathclose {}}, and the second case verifies that J is inhabited, so a is positive.

frontmatter.title: When is a continuous pointed dcpo positively generated?

mainmatter.p: We describe some light conditions under which a continuous dcpo is positively generated.

frontmatter.title: Basis with decidable bottomhood yields positive generation

mainmatter.p: Let A be a pointed continuous dcpo presented by a basis B \subseteq A for each element b \in B it is decidable whether b= \bot. Then A is positively generated.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: We observe that every element of B is non-negatively generated, so we may conclude that A is positively generated.

frontmatter.title: Positively generated basis yields positive generation

mainmatter.p: Let A be a pointed continuous dcpo presented by a basis B \subseteq A whose elements are all positively generated in A. Then A is positively generated.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: As positive generation implies non-negative generation for an element of a pointed dcpo, we may conclude that A is positively generated.

mainmatter.p: Let x,y:D be two elements of a dcpo D; we say that x is way below y, written x \ll y, when for any directed subset I \subseteq D such that y \sqsubseteq \bigsqcup {I}, there exists z \in I such that x \sqsubseteq z.
An element a of a pointed dcpo A is called non-negatively generated when a \sqsubseteq \bigsqcup _{ \lnot { \mathopen {} \left ( a= \bot \right ) \mathclose {}} }a holds.
When a pointed dcpo A satisfies either of the following equivalent conditions, we will refer to it as positively generated.

ol.li: Every element a: A is positively generated, i.e. we have a = \bigsqcup _{p: \mathsf { pos } \, a}a.
For any semidirected subset I \subseteq A, we have \bigsqcup I = \bigsqcup { \mathopen {} \left \{ a \in I \mid \mathsf { pos } \, a \right \} \mathclose {}}.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: (2) implies (1) by setting I = { \mathopen {} \left \{ a \right \} \mathclose {}}. Conversely, assume that each element is the supremum of its positive part and fix a semidirected subset I \subseteq A. We compute:
block.tex: \bigsqcup { \mathopen {} \left \{ a \in I \mid \mathsf { pos } \, a \right \} \mathclose {}} = \bigsqcup _{a \in { \mathopen {} \left \{ a \in I \mid \mathsf { pos } \, a \right \} \mathclose {}} }a = \bigsqcup _{a \in I} \bigsqcup _{p \in \mathsf { pos } \, a} a = \bigsqcup _{a \in I} a

mainmatter.p: A dcpo is called pointed when it has a bottom element \bot under the information order.

frontmatter.title: Basis with decidable bottomhood yields positive generation

mainmatter.p: Let A be a pointed continuous dcpo presented by a basis B \subseteq A for each element b \in B it is decidable whether b= \bot. Then A is positively generated.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: We observe that every element of B is non-negatively generated, so we may conclude that A is positively generated.

frontmatter.title: Positively generated basis yields positive generation

mainmatter.p: Let A be a pointed continuous dcpo presented by a basis B \subseteq A whose elements are all positively generated in A. Then A is positively generated.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: As positive generation implies non-negative generation for an element of a pointed dcpo, we may conclude that A is positively generated.
